mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime that suppresses discount rates on equity valuations, producing sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2018-01-15 indicates near-zero rate environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 100,000 persons signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate demand contraction.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 persons in a single month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite payroll decline, or the correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and future earnings compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations contract in anticipation of lower corporate revenues.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances despite consecutive retail sales declines, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) growth rate decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while remaining positive signals a supply-side slowdown that constrains profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth rate falls below 0.5% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of triggered instances despite supply deceleration, or the lagged correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month signals broad-based demand expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward. The mechanism requires both series to accelerate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month by at least 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 760 thousand units and RSAFS at 403,929 million as of 2018-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.3%) and unchanged or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) signals monetary policy lag that erodes real returns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors demand higher equity risk premiums.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 228.723 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2018-01-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag during inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite inflation-rate divergence, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that precedes demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market anticipates earnings compression from both lower consumer spending and potential recession.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.3% as of 2018-01-15 indicates elevated unemployment
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of observations when UNRATE > 4.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE level and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate while payroll employment (PAYEMS) remains flat or declining signals late-cycle stagnation that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice terminal growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2% while PAYEMS shows no month-over-month growth for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13558.0 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2017-10-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of triggered instances despite synchronized GDP and payroll deceleration, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
